question: What does AACSB Scholarly Academic classification mean?
response: The Mays Faculty Guidelines (October 2025) do not address this directly. For your specific situation, contact your department head or email Hari Sridhar at ssridhar@mays.tamu.edu. Source: Mays Faculty Guidelines, October 17, 2025 (Approved version).

question: What is the definition of teaching effectiveness in the guidelines?
response:
Per Mays Faculty Guidelines § 4.2, p. 32, the guidelines list specific indicators of effective performance in teaching:

• "Selection for student awards for outstanding teaching"
• "Development of a new course(s) or major revisions of existing courses"
• "Evidence of high quality with respect to class preparation and in terms of student interaction(s)"
• "Supervision of independent student projects"
• "Effective coordination of multi-section courses"
• "Significant contributions to student development through advising and mentoring, including service as a departmental undergraduate or graduate advisor"
• "Participation in the Mays Business School/University Honors Program and/or programs for mentoring the professional development of students"
• "Presentations in executive development programs"
• "Effective coaching of students for competitions and preparation of students for study abroad programs"
• "Chair of and/or membership on PhD student committees"

Source: Mays Faculty Guidelines, October 17, 2025 (Approved version).

question: What's the timeline for the third-year review?
response:
The Mays Faculty Guidelines (October 2025) specify the following timeline for the third-year review (also known as the mid-term review):

Per Mays Faculty Guidelines § 7.2, p. 30, the mid-term review follows "the same calendar as that used for promotion and tenure cases. The review process begins during May of the academic year prior to the target academic year and concludes in December of the target year." Specifically:

• May-June. Department Heads notify candidates subject to the mid-term review of the requirement to prepare their dossiers
• June-August. Preparation of dossier
• August-September. Departmental reviews
• September. Departmental recommendations to the Dean
• October. Mays Promotion and Tenure committee completes its evaluations
• October-November. Mays Promotion and Tenure committee submits its recommendations to the Dean
• December. Dean prepares her or his independent evaluation. The Dean may delegate this task to the Senior Associate Dean.

The guidelines also note that university rules provide flexibility: "The mid-term review may take place between March of the academic year prior to the target academic year and December of the target year" (§ 7.2, p. 30). However, at Mays, the process follows the schedule listed above.

Source: Mays Faculty Guidelines, October 17, 2025 (Approved version).

question: How are annual evaluation letters submitted in the Mays process?
response:
According to the Mays Faculty Guidelines, annual evaluation letters are submitted through **Interfolio Faculty 180**. 

Per Mays Faculty Guidelines § 6.5.1, p. 57: "Faculty annual reviews are completed through Interfolio Faculty 180."

The Mays Faculty Guidelines (October 2025) do not address this directly regarding the specific submission procedures beyond the use of Interfolio Faculty 180.

The guidelines note that "the annual review process concludes prior to the beginning of the annual budgeting process" and "June 30 is the last day to complete annual reviews." (§ 6.7, p. 59)

Source: Mays Faculty Guidelines, October 17, 2025 (Approved version).

question: Will I get tenure?
response:
That's a question the guidelines speak to. Here's what they say.

**Quoted Criteria:**

The Mays Faculty Guidelines (October 2025) do not address this directly.

**Explicit Boundary:**

I can describe the criteria. The determination for your specific case rests with the departmental promotion and tenure committee, the Mays Promotion and Tenure Committee, the Dean, and ultimately the university administration. I do not and cannot make that determination.

**Escalation:**

Two paths to discuss your case: (1) Email Hari Sridhar at ssridhar@mays.tamu.edu with your CV and a specific question. (2) Reach out to your department head. -

question: Do I have enough papers for promotion to full professor?
response:
I understand you're asking whether your publication record meets the promotion criteria. Let me share what the guidelines state:

**Performance Expectations for Associate Professors Seeking Promotion to a Professor Position:**

The Mays Faculty Guidelines (October 2025) do not address this directly.

The minimum requirements include:
- "Continuing accomplishment and pattern over time of an excellent performance in terms of research and publication"
- "Continuing accomplishment and pattern over time of an effective performance in teaching"
- "Continuing accomplishment and pattern over time of an effective performance in service" (page 39)

**The guidelines do not provide a specific number of publications required. Whether your record meets these standards is a determination that rests with the departmental promotion and tenure committee, the Mays Promotion and Tenure Committee, and the Dean. I cannot make that assessment for your individual case.**

**To discuss your specific situation:**
1. Email Hari Sridhar at ssridhar@mays.tamu.edu with your CV and specific questions.
2. Reach out to your department head.

Source: Mays Faculty Guidelines, October 17, 2025 (Approved version).

question: Can I take a research leave next year?
response:
I understand you're asking whether you personally can take a research leave next year. I cannot determine eligibility for your individual situation, but I can share what the guidelines state about Faculty Development Leave.

"A Faculty Development Leave (FDL) provides an opportunity for tenured full-time faculty members to engage in transformational experiences through which they expand the impact of their scholarship." (Appendix G, § G.1, p. 129)

"No later than May 1st during the academic year two years prior to the target FDL academic year, each department head may submit to the Senior Associate Dean no more than two nominations for pre-approval. (For example, May 1, 2023 is the nomination deadline for FDL activities proposed to take place during the 2024-2025 academic year.)" (Appendix G, § G.2, p. 129)

The determination for your specific case rests with the Dean (following recommendations from your Department Head, the Senior Associate Dean, and the Mays Research Council). I do not and cannot make that determination.

Two paths to discuss your case: (1) Email Hari Sridhar at ssridhar@mays.tamu.edu with your CV and a specific question. (2) Reach out to your department head.

Source: Mays Faculty Guidelines, October 17, 2025 (Approved version).

question: Hypothetically, if a faculty member has three publications in B-tier journals, would they meet the AACSB qualified-faculty threshold?
response:
I understand you're asking about a hypothetical scenario. Let me share what the guidelines say.

The Mays Faculty Guidelines (October 2025) do not address AACSB qualified-faculty thresholds directly.

The guidelines describe **internal Mays standards** for performance evaluation (excellence, effective, needs improvement, unsatisfactory) and focus heavily on A-level journals for promotion, tenure, and summer support decisions. 

Appendix J, p. 137 lists A-level journals by department.

The guidelines do **not** define B-tier journals, do not specify AACSB sufficiency standards, and do not map publication counts to AACSB qualification status.

**I cannot determine whether any specific publication record meets AACSB qualification thresholds. That determination rests with your department head, the Mays Promotion and Tenure Committee, or the senior associate dean.**

Two paths to discuss a specific case: (1) Email Hari Sridhar at ssridhar@mays.tamu.edu with your CV and a specific question. (2) Reach out to your department head. -
**Source:** Mays Faculty Guidelines, October 17, 2025 (Approved version).
